regions.text: The more the parts of a thing need one another, the more complex it is. Take one little wheel out of a watch and see if the other parts can work without it.
Which is more complex—a stone or a plant? The parts of the plant work together to keep it alive. Each part is dependent upon the others.
The parts of a stone do not work in this way for one another. A stone is much simpler than a plant.
Any living thing, however small or simple it may seem, is far more complex than anything which has no life.
